topic.name: Tax Incentives for Energy and Manufacturing
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to tax credits and deductions aimed at promoting investment in energy and manufacturing sectors.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the energy tax credit for investment in solar energy property.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to establish tax-exempt manufacturing reinvestment accounts (MRAs) for taxpayers engaged in a manufacturing business.
Permits expenditures from an MRA for expenses for property to be used in the manufacturing business and expenses for employee job training and workforce development.
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the tax credit for qualified fuel cell property.

topic.name: Medicare System Reforms
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at expanding and enhancing the Medicare program for beneficiaries.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to allow any Medicare beneficiary to enter into a contract with a non-participating physician.
Requires a Medicare beneficiary to agree in writing in such a contract to pay the physician or practitioner for a Medicare-covered item or service.
Document 2: Amends part A of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to authorize an option for any citizen or permanent resident of the United States to buy into Medicare.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover pharmacist services.

topic.name: Education Policy and Financial Aid
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving financial aid accessibility and educational support services for students in both higher education and K-12 settings.

Document 1: Simplifying Financial Aid for Students Act of 2014 - Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires the Secretary of Education to provide for the use of data from the second preceding tax year when...
Document 2: Full-Service Community Schools Act of 2011 - Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
to assist public elementary or secondary schools to function as full-service community schools
Document 3: Promoting Readiness through Early Pell Act of 2016 or the PREP Act of 2016
amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 by allowing a Federal Pell Grant to be awarded to a student who is dually or concurrently enrolled

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, specifically regarding the temporary suspension of duties on various goods.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on cermets for ceramic discharge lamps.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2,7-Naphthalenedisulfonic acid
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic staple fibers
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving services, support, and care for veterans in the United States.

Document 1: Increases the amount of monthly subsistence allowance payable to veterans participating in the veterans' vocational rehabilitation program
pay such veteran a subsistence allowance for three months
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse any full-time board-certified health professional for continuing professional education expenses
expenses required to maintain licensure
Document 3: prohibits any Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) senior executive employee from being paid a bonus unless the VA determines that no permanent VA medical center director position has been vacant for 18 consecutive months
the VA shall report to Congress on the number and length of vacancies in VA medical center director positions

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Leasing
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing the exploration and production of oil and gas in offshore areas of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to direct the Secretary of the Interior to include in a specified schedule of proposed lease sales.
Authorizes the governor of South Carolina to petition the Secretary to make available for leasing any portion of the area.
Document 2: Requires holders of leases authorizing exploration and production of oil or natural gas to develop them diligently.
Amends the Mineral Leasing Act and the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior.
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to revoke existing presidential authority to withdraw any unleased lands.
Authorizes a state governor to petition the Secretary of the Interior to issue leases for oil and natural gas exploration.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. immigration laws and policies, including temporary work visas and health-related entry restrictions.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category
Authorizes such alien to petition to become a lawful permanent resident
Document 2: Makes the E-Verify Program permanent and mandatory
Revises specified Program procedures
Document 3: Prohibits a consular officer from issuing an immigrant or nonimmigrant visa
An alien from being admitted into the United States if the alien's journey originated in a designated country

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on monitoring and reporting the development of electromagnetic pulse weapons by foreign nations.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Area Designation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation and management of wilderness areas in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado
Designates specified lands in Colorado managed by the BLM or the U.S. Forest Service as wilderness
Document 2: Designates specified Bureau of Land Management (BLM) land in Oregon as potential wilderness areas
Provides for the designation of such areas as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System

topic.name: Violence and Exploitation Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing violence and exploitation, particularly against vulnerable populations such as women and children.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act to include sex trafficking as a target of grants to combat violent crime against Indian women.
Grants to address the needs of youth who are victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, and sex trafficking.
Document 2: Imposes mandatory minimum prison terms for possession of child pornography and increases penalties for human trafficking crimes.
Expands restitution for certain child sex offenses and includes production of child pornography within illicit sexual conduct.
Document 3: Amends the Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act to require consultation with state and local officials before awarding grants for housing unaccompanied alien children.

topic.name: Mental Health Services for Older Adults
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving mental health services and outreach for older adults, particularly through integration into primary care and addressing substance abuse issues.

Document 1: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings
support the establishment and maintenance of interdisciplinary geriatric mental health outreach teams in community settings where older adults reside
Document 3: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities for projects to demonstrate ways of integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings
designate as a project of national significance the treatment of older adults with substance-related disorders

topic.name: Access to Medical Treatments
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to investigational medical treatments and addressing issues related to opioid abuse and drug sentencing.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to permit an investigational drug, biological product, or device to be made available for expanded access

Establishes a procedure for accelerated approval of an investigational drug, biological product, or device that is reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit to a patient suffering from a serious or life-threatening condition.
Document 2: HHS must advance education and awareness regarding the risk of abuse of prescription opioids if they are not taken as prescribed
The Office of National Drug Control Policy may award grants to implement community-wide strategies to address a high rate of, or sudden increase in, opioid or methamphetamine abuse.

topic.name: Water Rights and Ecosystem Restoration
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at managing water rights and restoring ecosystems across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: Bill Williams River Water Rights Settlement Act of 2014
confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement
Document 2: San Gabriel Watershed Restoration Act
prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration
Document 3: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007
carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality

topic.name: Mortgage Assistance and Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing support and restructuring options for homeowners facing mortgage challenges.

Document 1: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Prohibits the GSEs from charging a mortgagee any up-front fee beyond the standard guarantee fee for the refinancing of the qualified mortgage.
Document 2: Establishes a Family Foreclosure Rescue Corporation to purchase home mortgage loans from any financial institution.
Requires the Secretary to implement a plan that seeks to maximize loan restructuring on a sustainable basis and to minimize foreclosures.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to require specified mortgagees to indemnify HUD for payment of a mortgage insurance claim if the mortgage was not materially originated or underwritten in accordance with HUD requirements.
Authorizes the Secretary to terminate approval to originate or underwrite single-family mortgages of any mortgagee with an excessive rate of early defaults and claims.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation and Research
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at enhancing the conservation, research, and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program
directs NOAA to submit to Congress a national coral reef action strategy.
Document 2: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 3: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds
NOAA must also submit an international coral reef ecosystem strategy to Congress.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modifying agricultural support programs and financial protections for various sectors within the agriculture industry.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat
Document 2: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
establish a dairy producer margin insurance program to protect dairy producer income
Document 3: Suspends specified agricultural price support authorities
regard to covered commodities, peanuts, sugar, and milk

topic.name: Energy Efficiency and Renewable Technology
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving energy efficiency and promoting renewable energy technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Gas Turbine Efficiency Act of 2009 directs the Secretary of Energy to improve gas turbine efficiency.
Requires technology demonstration through component testing and field demonstrations.
Document 2: Marine and Hydrokinetic Renewable Energy Promotion Act of 2011 requires research and development for marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy.
Establishes a grant program to advance the development of marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy.

topic.name: Data Breach and Privacy Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and regulations governing the notification and protection of personally identifiable information in the event of data breaches.

Document 1: Requires any agency or business entity with sensitive personally identifiable information to notify without unreasonable delay any U.S. resident of a security breach
Requires an agency or business entity to give notice of a security breach to any affected individuals
Document 2: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable protected health information
Makes any person who violates this Act liable to any person whose personally identifiable covered information has been disclosed.
Document 3: Requires commercial entities that acquire, maintain, store, or utilize personal information to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data

Requires a covered entity that owns or licenses such data to give notice of any breach of security that the entity reasonably believes has caused or will cause identity theft or other actual financial harm.

topic.name: Anti-Money Laundering and Identity Theft Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at preventing money laundering, identity theft, and related financial crimes.

Document 1: Revises certain requirements for anti-money laundering programs
Shields an institution from liability for the noncompliance of a money transmitting business
Document 2: Directs the Department of Justice (DOJ) to establish procedures to expedite the review of identity theft cases
seek an indictment in any case involving identity theft or aggravated identity theft
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term on any person who willfully misappropriates another person's taxpayer identity
enhance requirements relating to electronic filing of tax returns

topic.name: Banking and Financial Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating banking practices and capital requirements for financial institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956
prohibits financial holding companies and national banks from engaging in real estate brokerage or management activities.
Document 2: directs federal banking agencies to study and report the impact of capital requirements
specifies Dodd-Frank provisions to be included.
Document 3: amends the Dodd-Frank Act concerning capital requirements
exempts regulated insurance entities from minimum capital requirements.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at the conservation and protection of various wildlife species and their habitats.

Document 1: Amends the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 to make nonhuman primates a prohibited wildlife species
Sets forth civil and criminal penalties for violations of the requirements of this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act to authorize the Department of the Interior to increase the price of each Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp
An increase in price shall take effect at least two years after the effective date of the last price increase.
Document 3: Establishes the Great Cats and Rare Canids Conservation Fund as a separate account of the Multinational Species Conservation Fund
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to use Fund amounts to provide assistance for projects for the conservation of rare felids and rare canids.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Measures
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures and acts related to financial regulation and postal service designations in the United States.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3031 Veterans Road West in Staten Island, New York, as the 'Leonard Montalto Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Directs the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to study and report to Congress on the likely effects that differences between the United States and other jurisdictions in implementing the derivatives credit valuation adjustment capital requirement would have upon U.S. financial institutions.

topic.name: Railroad and Transportation Safety
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety measures and regulations in railroad and transportation systems.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to provide railroad safety grants
study and report on best practices for railroad safety
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to study the cost and impact of rerouting freight rail traffic
avoid its transportation through urban areas
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to issue regulations to allow natural gas vehicles
operate on the Interstate Highway System

topic.name: Federal Science and Technology Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving and regulating federal research and standards in science and technology fields.

Document 1: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to prohibit the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) from approving the renewal of a nuclear reactor license.
Prohibits the renewal application if submitted more than 10 years before the license's expiration.
Document 2: Establishes a National Forensic Science Research Initiative to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.
The National Science Foundation (NSF) shall contract with the National Research Council to develop a report identifying critical challenges in forensic science.
Document 3: Directs the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a STEM Inclusion Interagency Working Group.
The NSF shall strengthen the goals and activities of the ADVANCE program to focus on increasing the participation of women in STEM.

topic.name: Retirement Savings Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing and regulating retirement savings options for individuals and small businesses.

Document 1: allows funds paid to an individual to aid in the pursuit of graduate or postdoctoral study or research to be saved in an Individual Retirement Account (IRA)
considered compensation for purposes of current law provisions that limit annual deductible IRA contributions
Document 2: determination of a federal firefighter's average pay for purposes of computing retirement and annuity benefits
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to employer-established simple individual retirement accounts (IRAs) and pension plans
establish automatic deferral IRAs to permit the automatic enrollment of employees earning at least $5,000

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at supporting small businesses through various initiatives, including funding goals and disaster assistance.

Document 1: increase the government-wide goal for awards to small businesses from 23% to 24%
increase the goal for award to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans
Document 2: direct the National Women's Business Council to conduct at least one annual study
revise matching funds requirements for women's business center projects
Document 3: amends the Small Business Act with respect to obtaining the best available collateral for a disaster loan
expresses the sense of Congress that the Administrator should reduce paperwork burdens on small businesses applying for SBA disaster assistance loans

topic.name: Public Health and Tribal Affairs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative actions related to public health initiatives, particularly concerning gestational diabetes and the involvement of Native American tribes in health and environmental management.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to develop a multisite gestational diabetes research project
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to expand and intensify public health research on gestational diabetes.
Document 2: Amends various enactments relating to Native Americans to make technical corrections
Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to include territories and Indian tribes as eligible grant recipients under a program that addresses public safety.

topic.name: Social Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the protection and enhancement of Social Security benefits for retirees and individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: prohibits increases in retirement age
prevents reductions in benefit payment amounts
Document 2: directs the publication of a Consumer Price Index specifically for elderly consumers
amends OASDI to use new CPI for cost-of-living increases
Document 3: eliminates waiting period for disability benefits for terminally ill
addresses undue hardship for terminally ill individuals

topic.name: Regulatory Economic Impact Assessment
topic.description: This topic encompasses the evaluation and categorization of the economic effects of federal regulations on various sectors, including small businesses and employment.

Document 1: Requires a federal agency... to assign the rule to, and publish in the Federal Register, the following category that applies based on the highest possible annual effect
Defines 'significant economic impact' for purposes of provisions concerning initial and final regulator flexibility analyses as an annual economic effect of at least $100 million.
Document 2: This bill bars any major rule proposed by a federal agency... until the Department of Labor... conducts a study to determine how the rule will impact wage growth... and employment in each such industry.
Document 3: Requires the Environmental Protection Agency... to timely provide on the request of a state... the following in order to comply with the regulatory action: (1) technical support, (2) data and data modeling, and (3) clarification and guidance.

topic.name: Iran Nuclear Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: The documents focus on U.S. legislative measures related to sanctions and agreements concerning Iran's nuclear program and its implications for international security.

Document 1: requires the President to reinstate immediately all sanctions against Iran
if the Director of National Intelligence certifies that Iran has acquired nuclear weapons technology from North Korea.
Document 2: prohibits the use of funds by the United States to make a payment to the government of Iran
until the President certifies to Congress that Iran has paid all compensatory damages awarded to a U.S. person.
Document 3: amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to direct the President regarding agreements with Iran
ensures that Iran's permitted nuclear activities will not be used to further any nuclear-related military purpose.

topic.name: Veterans Economic Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and programs aimed at providing economic opportunities and support services for veterans, particularly focusing on housing and rehabilitation.

Document 1: qualify low-income building units that provide housing for a student who previously was a homeless child or youth for the low-income housing tax credit.
Document 2: Establishes in the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) a Veterans Economic Opportunity Administration
to administer VA programs of economic opportunity assistance to veterans and their dependents and survivors.
Document 3: Allows homeless veterans' comprehensive services grant funds to be used to provide funding for service center staff.
direct the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to annually increase the rate of such payment to reflect anticipated changes in the cost of furnishing services.

topic.name: Election Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign finance practices to enhance transparency and reduce the influence of money in politics.

Document 1: Makes it unlawful for a person during specified periods before a federal primary or general election to make certain prerecorded political telephone calls
requires immediate disclosure of the recorded nature of the calls, the identity of the caller, and the appearance of the caller's telephone number
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to the use of campaign contributions
applies the prohibition against the conversion to personal use of contributions accepted by candidates also to contributions accepted by political committees
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the system of public financing for presidential primary and general elections
increases the amount of matching funds for presidential primaries from a one-to-one match to a six-to-one match for contributions of $250 or less

topic.name: Consumer Credit Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating consumer credit practices and protecting consumer rights in financial transactions.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require conspicuous signage regarding credit and charge card accounts.
Details requirements for disclosures on credit card applications.
Document 2: Prohibits certain actions by federal agencies regarding credit card rules.
Mandates clear disclosures for credit card terms and conditions.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act regarding the definition of debt collectors.

topic.name: Public Safety Communications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing public safety communication systems and access to emergency broadcasting.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a satellite carrier to retransmit digital signals of noncommercial educational television stations.
Requires satellite carriers to provide access to public television programming.
Document 2: Requires the FCC to develop a program for continued broadcasting of public safety information in analog television service.
Addresses emergency readiness and transition information during the digital television transition.
Document 3: Establishes the First Responders Interoperable Device Availability Trust Fund.
Requires grants for interoperable devices for public safety personnel.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs and Benefits
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to the benefits and service conditions for veterans and military personnel.

Document 1: amends the Foreign Service Act of 1980
require Foreign Service employees overseas to serve at a post for between four and six consecutive years.
Document 2: provides transportation on scheduled and unscheduled military flights
for veterans with a service-connected, permanent disability rated as total
Document 3: entitles to educational assistance under the Montgomery GI Bill
Establishes the duration of such assistance (in most cases 36 months)

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing public health and safety through the regulation of drinking water quality and consumer product safety.

Document 1: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require monitoring of unregulated contaminants
Requires the Administrator to evaluate data accessibility for the public.
Document 2: Requires federal agencies to implement public information plans for product safety
Establishes a clearinghouse for product recall and safety information.
Document 3: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to provide assistance to public water systems
Increases funds reserved for technical assistance to disadvantaged communities.

topic.name: Labor Relations and Employee Rights
topic.description: Legislation focused on the rights of employees and the processes governing labor relations, including collective bargaining and program audits for military transition assistance.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Labor to enter into a contract for audits of the Transition Assistance Program
Requires such audits at least once every three years.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB).
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to revise requirements for National Labor Relation Board (NLRB) determination of an appropriate bargaining unit
Prohibits exclusion of employees from the unit unless the interests of the group seeking a separate unit are sufficiently distinct.

topic.name: Detention and Interrogation Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at reforming the detention and interrogation practices of the U.S. government, particularly concerning detainees at Guantanamo Bay and the use of private contractors in intelligence operations.

Document 3: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to require the Director of National Intelligence to prepare an annual assessment for the intelligence community.
Prohibits the use of private contractors for the arrest, interrogation, detention, or transportation or transfer of persons under government custody or control.

topic.name: Education Funding and Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the allocation of educational funds and policies affecting local educational agencies and higher education institutions.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to allot funds to states for distribution to local educational agencies (LEAs)
to recruit, hire, and train 100,000 new classroom paraprofessionals
Document 2: This bill amends title IV (Student Aid) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
to make an institution of higher education (IHE) that is a sanctuary campus ineligible for funds under title IV.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA)
to revise provisions for military recruiter access to secondary school student information.

topic.name: Voting Rights and Redistricting
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring fair voting practices and the establishment of independent redistricting processes to prevent gerrymandering.

Document 1: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
Requires any congressional redistricting to be conducted according to a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission
Document 2: Require each state with voter registration requirements relating to federal elections to permit any eligible individual on the day of a federal election to register to vote
cast a vote, in such election.
Document 3: Prohibits any state whose congressional districts have been redistricted after a decennial census from carrying out another redistricting until after the next apportionment
enforce the Voting Rights Act of 1965.

topic.name: Firearm Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to the regulation, possession, and use of firearms in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Credit Card Accountability Responsibility and Disclosure Act of 2009 to repeal the provisions relating to the carrying of certain firearms in units of the National Park System and the National Wildlife Refuge System.
Guns-Free National Parks Act of 2012
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to reinstate the Public Safety and Recreational Firearms Use Protection Act's assault weapons ban.
Prohibit the manufacture, transfer, or possession of a semiautomatic assault weapon or a large capacity ammunition feeding device.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary concerned to authorize an eligible member of the Armed Forces assigned to an Armed Forces recruitment center to carry a service-issue sidearm as a personal- or force-protection measure while on duty.
This Act shall not be interpreted to grant any member of the Armed Forces the authority to conduct civilian law enforcement functions within U.S. territory.

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving safety standards and operational regulations within the aviation industry.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for operations.
Directs the FAA Administrator to establish a program to utilize undergraduate and technical colleges in research on subjects of relevance to the FAA.
Document 2: Directs the Department of Transportation to issue regulations to establish minimum dimensions for passenger seats on aircraft.
Regulations for the safety and health of passengers.

Document 3: Directs the Federal Aviation Administration to issue regulations necessary to prohibit the use of leaded fuel by aircraft.

topic.name: Food Safety and Labeling Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving food safety and nutrition labeling standards in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to authorize a food service establishment to provide nutrition information
Requires the labeling of a standard food item served or offered for sale in a food service establishment
Document 2: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act to permit an entity that purchases carcasses to perform further examination
Prohibits a person, firm, or corporation from preventing such further examination
Document 3: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to promulgate a final regulation revising federal nutrition labeling regulations
Require the label of a food that has less than .5 grams of trans fat per serving to express that information with an asterisk

topic.name: Native Governance and Recognition
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legal frameworks and acts related to the recognition and governance of Native populations in the United States, particularly focusing on their rights to self-governance and compensation.

Document 1: Establishes the Native Hawaiian Interagency Coordinating Group.
Recognizes the right of the qualified Native Hawaiian constituents to reorganize the single Native Hawaiian governing entity.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to report to the appropriate congressional committees regarding the economic situation in the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.
Extends by one year the date for beginning the transition period of U.S. immigration laws to the CNMI.
Document 3: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages to organize as Urban Corporations.
to receive certain settlement lands and other compensation pursuant to this Act.

topic.name: Servicemembers' Legal Protections
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the legal and financial protections for servicemembers and their families.

Document 1: extends Consumer Financial Protection Bureau oversight
provisions under the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act
Document 2: amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act
custody order that was in effect preceding such deployment shall be reinstated
Document 3: extends the 6% interest rate limitation on debt
debt entered into during a servicemember's military service

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, policies, and regulations of the United States Postal Service.

Document 1: amends the Tariff Act of 1930
impose a duty of $1 on each item of non-letter class mail
Document 2: policy of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to ensure reasonable and sustainable workloads
changes in pay policies and schedules
Document 3: Prohibits the United States Postal Service (USPS) from closing any post office
results in more than 10 miles distance between any 2 post offices

topic.name: Crime Prevention and Justice Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing crime prevention strategies and reforming justice-related standards.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to authorize the Director of the Bureau of Justice Assistance to waive matching fund requirements applicable to the grant program for the purchase of armor vests for law enforcement officers in the case of fiscal hardship.
Bulletproof Vest Partnership Grant Act of 2008 - Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to extend such program through FY2012.
Document 2: This bill amends the federal criminal code to modify the criminal liability standard for certain disclosures of national defense information.
This bill eliminates gross negligence as an element the criminal offense to impose strict liability on a person who permits the loss or removal of national defense information.
Document 3: Prevention Resources for Eliminating Criminal Activity Using Tailored Interventions in Our Neighborhoods Act of 2007 or the PRECAUTION Act - Establishes the National Commission on Public Safety Through Crime Prevention.

Authorizes the Director of the National Institute of Justice to make three-year grants to public and private entities for the implementation and evaluation of innovative crime or delinquency prevention or intervention strategies.

topic.name: Abortion Regulation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating abortion access and funding in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
Document 2: prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
including any abortion procedure
Document 3: prohibit the Secretary of Health and Human Service from providing any federal family planning assistance to an entity unless the entity certifies that it will not perform an abortion
excludes an abortion where the pregnancy is the result of an act of rape or incest against a minor

topic.name: Public Infrastructure and Safety Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at enhancing public safety and infrastructure through funding and regulation.

Document 1: Metropolitan Medical Response System Program Act of 2009 - assists state and local governments in preparing for and responding to public health and mass casualty incidents.
Authorizes grants to state and local governments for coordinated response to mass casualty incidents.
Document 2: Child Care Construction and Renovation Act of 2007 - authorizes the use of community development block grants to construct and renovate child care facilities.
Provides technical and financial assistance to eligible child care providers.

topic.name: Military Justice and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on military justice reforms and protections against retaliation for reporting misconduct.

Document 1: Prohibits federal assistance to a country that does not prohibit: shark finning in its territorial waters;
the importation, sale, or possession, of shark fins obtained by shark finning.
Document 2: Amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice to require, when a court-martial sentence that resulted in forfeiture of pay and allowances is later set aside or disapproved
that the restoration of the forfeited amounts be adjusted for inflation.
Document 3: this bill amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice to subject to punishment by a court-martial any person who, with the intent to retaliate against any individual for reporting a criminal offense
DOD personnel who investigate claims of retaliation in connection with reports of sexual assault in the Armed Forces shall receive training on the nature and consequences of sexual assault trauma.

topic.name: District of Columbia Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to the governance, budget autonomy, and legal establishment of offices within the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Establish the Office of the District Attorney for the District of Columbia (DA)
Prohibits the DA from engaging directly or indirectly in private practice.
Document 2: Authorize the Secretary of the Army to accept and expend funds contributed by non-federal public entities
Ensure that all final permit decisions carried out using such funds are made available to the public.
Document 3: Eliminate all federally-imposed mandates over the District of Columbia's local budget process
Repeals the federal cap on amounts the District may borrow.

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Manufacturing Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing U.S. trade practices and supporting domestic manufacturing competitiveness.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 with respect to aviation smuggling
extends its coverage of aircraft to ultralight vehicles
Document 2: Amends the Buy American Act to require each federal department to submit a report
evaluation of contractor compliance with the provisions of that Act
Document 3: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to allow U.S. manufacturers to participate in duty proceedings
requires the U.S. International Trade Commission to weigh harm to industrial users

topic.name: U.S.-Africa Relations and Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving health care capacity and fostering trade and development relations between the United States and Africa.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act to authorize assistance for human health care capacity in sub-Saharan Africa.
Directs the President to develop a strategy for coordinating assistance programs.
Document 2: Directs the President to appoint a Special Representative for United States-Africa Trade, Development, and Diaspora Affairs.
Promote U.S.-African trade and investment relations and foster socioeconomic development.
Document 3: Amends the United States Leadership Against HIV/AIDS, Tuberculosis, and Malaria Act.
Eliminates the HIV/AIDS abstinence-until marriage funding set-aside.

topic.name: Family and Medical Leave Policies
topic.description: Legislation related to employee leave entitlements for family-related circumstances, including bereavement and care for dependents.

Document 1: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to entitle an eligible employee to up to 12 workweeks of leave during any 12-month period because of the death of a son or daughter.
Allows such an employee to substitute any available paid leave for any leave without pay.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to carry out a demonstration program to award five-year grants to eligible local education agencies for training on effective ways to teach, communicate, and support children with autism spectrum disorders.
Implementing a program of parental support and involvement in the education of students with such disorders.
Document 3: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to provide for employee leave to care for a domestic partner or his or her child, parent-in-law, adult child, sibling, grandparent, grandchild, son-in-law, or daughter-in-law.
Amends federal civil service law to apply the same leave allowance to federal employees.

topic.name: Tax-Exempt Bonds and Financing
topic.description: Legislation related to tax-exempt bonds for public projects and renewable energy initiatives.

Document 1: Build America Bonds Act of 2012
make permanent the issuance authority for Build America Bonds
Document 2: tax exemption for private activity bond interest
used for basic research
Document 3: Clean Renewable Energy Investment Act of 2010
tax-exempt new clean renewable energy bonds

topic.name: Employment Discrimination Protections
topic.description: Legislation aimed at prohibiting discrimination in employment and internship settings based on various characteristics including sexual orientation, gender identity, and other protected statuses.

Document 1: Prohibits certain employers from refusing to employ, discriminating against, or harassing interns because of age, race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, military status, disability, gender identity, predisposing genetic characteristics, marital status, or status as a victim of domestic violence.
Bars employers from compelling a pregnant intern to take a leave of absence.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of Defense from discriminating on the basis of sexual orientation against any member of the Armed Forces or any person seeking to become a member.
Authorizes the re-accession into the Armed Forces of otherwise qualified individuals previously separated for homosexuality, bisexuality, or homosexual conduct.
Document 3: Prohibits any federal employee from discriminating for or against any federal employee or applicant for federal employment on the basis of sexual orientation.
Affirms that discrimination against federal employees and applicants for federal employment on the basis of sexual orientation is already prohibited under current law.

topic.name: Commemoration of Military Service
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts and initiatives aimed at honoring and commemorating military service and sacrifice throughout American history.

Document 1: Establishes the World War I Centennial Commission to commemorate the centennial of World War I
facilitate and coordinate such activities throughout the United States
Document 2: may not adopt or enforce any policy that would restrict or prevent an association member from displaying a Service Flag
official banner authorized by the Department of Defense for display by families who have members serving in the Armed Forces
Document 3: establish a memorial on specified federal land in the District of Columbia to honor slaves and free black persons who served as soldiers
prohibits the National Mall Liberty Fund D.C. from using federal funds for the establishment of such memorial

topic.name: Youth Violence Prevention and Tax Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at preventing youth violence and substance abuse in schools, alongside tax amendments related to petroleum and alternative fuels.

Document 1: National Parents Corps program to promote safety and combat youth violence and substance abuse in public middle or secondary schools.
employ a Parent Leader to work on a full-time basis with students, other parents, and school officials to treat, prevent, and reduce violence and drug, alcohol, and tobacco addiction among students.
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the definition of 'crude oil' for purposes of the excise tax on petroleum and petroleum products.

topic.name: U.S. Human Rights Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting human rights and democracy in specific countries, particularly Vietnam and North Korea.

Document 1: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism until the President certifies to Congress that North Korea is no longer engaged in the illegal transfer of missile or nuclear technology.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should redesignate North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 2: Prohibits U.S. nonhumanitarian assistance to the government of Vietnam in excess of FY2014 amounts unless the President certifies to Congress that the government of Vietnam has made substantial progress respecting political, civil, media, Internet, and religious freedoms.

It is the sense of Congress that further easing of the prohibition on the sale of lethal military equipment to Vietnam shall require Vietnam to take additional and sustained steps to advance human rights protections.
Document 3: Prohibits the federal government from providing the government of Vietnam with nonhumanitarian development, trade, economic, and security assistance during any fiscal year that exceeds the amount of such assistance provided during FY2009, unless such increases are matched or exceeded by additional funding for programs focusing on human rights and democracy promotion.
Declares it is U.S. policy that educational and cultural exchange programs with Vietnam should promote progress toward freedom and democracy.

topic.name: Public Health and Defense Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to public health initiatives, immunization programs, and defense policies affecting military operations and procurement.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow states to purchase vaccines recommended for routine use
Requires group health plans to provide coverage for routine immunizations without cost sharing
Document 2: Provides for a tax exclusion of compensation paid to a member of the Armed Forces serving in Operation United Assistance
Requires that such service member undergo a program of not less than 21 days of controlled monitoring upon return from the outbreak area
Document 3: Prohibits funds from being used by the Department of Defense (DOD) on the KC-X (aerial refueling) tanker contract

Directs the Secretary of Defense and the contract source selection authority to require any prospective defense contractor or subcontractor not already covered by the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act to comply with such Act's requirements as a contract award condition

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions on Partnerships and Liability
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at enhancing international partnerships, particularly in defense, as well as regulations concerning liability and remittance transfers.

Document 1: the U.S.-India defense partnership is vital to regional and international stability and security
the President shall: annually assess the extent to which India possesses strategic operational capabilities
Document 2: require a remittance transfer provider, before initiating a transfer, to request from the sender of a remittance proof of the sender's status under U.S. immigration laws
impose, upon any sender unable to provide such proof of status, a fine equal to 7% of the U.S. dollar amount to be transferred
Document 3: exempts a lawful seller from liability for personal injury, monetary loss, or damage to property
limits the seller's liability to the personal injury, monetary loss, or damage to property directly caused by such activity

topic.name: Occupational Health and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing protections and standards for worker health and safety, particularly in the mining industry and for government employees.

Document 1: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Sets forth increased protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.
Document 2: Amends the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993 to give small miners 60 days to remedy their failure to timely file either a small miner's maintenance fee waiver application or an affidavit of annual labor.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that essential standards to protect miner health are out of date and congressional intervention is needed.

Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH) to forward to the Secretary of Labor its Recommended Exposure Limits for chemical and other hazards to miners.

topic.name: Employment Rights for Service Members
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the employment and reemployment rights of military service members and veterans, including protections and benefits during and after service.

Document 1: provides employment and reemployment rights for certain individuals ordered to full-time National Guard duty.
Document 2: amends provisions concerning uniformed services members' employment or reemployment rights or benefits
requires investigation and resolution of complaints to be completed no later than 90 days after receipt of the complaint.

topic.name: Sports Regulation and Gambling
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of sports betting, combat sports, and associated legal frameworks.

Document 1: Wire Clarification Act of 2010 - Amends the federal criminal code to provide that criminal penalties under such code and other provisions of federal law for transmitting bets and wages on sporting events or contests shall not apply to any bet or wager that is permissible under the Interstate Horseracing Act of 1978.
criminal penalties under such code and other provisions of federal law for transmitting bets and wages on sporting events
Document 2: Muhammad Ali Expansion Act - amends the Professional Boxing Safety Act of 1996 to establish definitions for 'fighter,' 'combat sport competition,' and 'mixed martial arts'
guidelines for minimum contractual provisions that should be included in bout agreements and mixed martial arts and other combat sport contracts
Document 3: Sports Gaming Opportunity Act of 2012 - Amends the federal judicial code to exempt a lottery, sweepstakes, or other betting, gambling, or wagering scheme authorized by a state
prohibition against a governmental entity, or a person acting pursuant to the law or compact of a governmental entity, sponsoring, operating, advertising, or promoting sports gambling

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Veterans Affairs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to U.S. foreign aid, economic development in conflict regions, and the employment and training of veterans returning from service.

Document 1: stimulate economic activity and development in Afghanistan
combat violent extremism
Document 2: Helping Iraq and Afghanistan Veterans Return to Employment
consideration of military training received by a veteran
Document 3: prohibits U.S. assistance for Afghanistan
work performed in Afghanistan by U.S. contractors is exempt from taxation

topic.name: U.S.-Cuba Energy Relations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to energy exploration and trade between the United States and Cuba, particularly focusing on hydrocarbon resources and telecommunications.

Document 1: engage in exploration and extraction of hydrocarbon resources from any portion of any foreign exclusive economic zone contiguous to the exclusive economic zone of the United States
amends the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act of 2006 to repeal the moratorium on oil and gas leasing
Document 2: repeal the embargo placed upon all trade with Cuba
permits installation and maintenance of telecommunications equipment and facilities in Cuba

topic.name: Child Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the legal framework for the protection of children from sexual exploitation and trafficking.

Document 1: Increases criminal penalties for offenses relating to the sexual exploitation of children and child pornography.
Requires the Secretary of State to deny passports for individuals convicted of sex offenses against children.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Commerce to include in each questionnaire a question to ascertain U.S. citizenship and immigration status.

topic.name: Mining and Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation, royalties, and reclamation associated with mining activities in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 to permit the use of moneys in the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund
to allow transportation and use of dredged materials for abandoned mine reclamation.
Document 2: Applies this Act to any mining claim, millsite claim, or tunnel site claim located under the general mining laws
Subjects production of locatable minerals to a royalty of 8% of the gross income from mining.
Document 3: amends the Mineral Leasing Act to require the coal lease royalty to be at least 12.5% of the assessment value of federal coal
establishes in the Treasury a Coal Area Economic Revitalization Fund for the annual deposit of $105 million of royalty revenues collected by the United States from coal leases.

topic.name: Compensation for Health Impacts
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to compensation for individuals affected by occupational hazards, including radiation exposure and black lung disease.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993 regarding waivers of small miner claim maintenance fees
qualifying for specified relief under the Act, including opportunity to cure.
Document 2: Amends the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act to expand the diseases for which individuals who were exposed to radiation from nuclear testing may claim compensation
Changes the presumption for a finding of cancer compensable under the program to a standard of clear and convincing evidence.
Document 3: Amends the Black Lung Benefits Act, with respect to claims filed on or after the effective date of the Black Lung Benefits Amendments of 1981
eliminate exceptions to the applicability of certain provisions regarding rebuttable presumptions.

topic.name: Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative acts aimed at promoting health, safety, and environmental considerations in professional and emergency contexts.

Document 1: Breastfeeding Promotion Act of 2011 - Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to include lactation as protected conduct.
Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act to extend the requirement that certain employers provide reasonable break time for an employee to express breast milk.
Document 2: Good Samaritan Health Professionals Act of 2011 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to provide that a health care professional shall not be liable under federal or state law for harm caused by any act or omission if: the professional is serving as a volunteer for purposes of responding to a disaster.
Makes exceptions where the harm was caused by an act or omission constituting willful or criminal misconduct.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the educational and ethical provisions for veterans and their services.

Document 1: veterans to use Department of Veterans Affairs post-9/11 educational assistance
independent study program at an educational institution
Document 2: Independent Ethics Commission Act of 2007
initiates investigations upon the filing of a complaint

topic.name: Legislative Amendments for Social and Economic Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments aimed at improving social welfare and economic measures, including tax deductions, financial reporting, and healthcare for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make the standard mileage rate used to determine the tax deduction for the use of a passenger automobile for charitable purposes equal to the standard mileage rate used for the tax deduction for business and moving expenses.
Increases such rate until December 31, 2008.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to direct the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to collect, publish, and keep current a set of indices that report the rate for dollar-denominated loans of various maturities.
Document 3: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to direct the Administration on Aging to carry out a program awarding grants to eligible entities to carry out six separate demonstration projects that focus on care coordination and service delivery for older individuals with chronic illness.

topic.name: Military Legislation and Veteran Affairs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation regarding military operations, veterans' benefits, and the management of military infrastructure.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine and/or prison term for profiteering and fraud involving a contract or the provision of goods or services in connection with a war, military action, or relief or reconstruction activities.
Subjects any property which constitutes or is derived from proceeds traceable to a violation of this Act to civil and criminal forfeiture.
Document 2: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to the Republic of the Philippines to provide benefits to persons who served in the organized military forces of the government of the Commonwealth of the Philippines.
Includes within the benefits to be provided: compensation for service-connected disabilities, medical care, education benefits, and death pensions.
Document 3: Requires the Department of Defense (DOD) to develop a force structure plan for the Armed Forces based on a DOD assessment of threats to U.S. national security.
DOD must certify that a round of installation actions will result in annual net savings within five years.

topic.name: Natural Disaster Mitigation and Research
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on studying and mitigating the impacts of natural disasters and climate change on the environment and infrastructure.

Document 3: Directs the Administrator of NOAA and the EPA to establish an Institute for Ocean and Coastal Adaptation to Climate Change.
Award grants to educational institutions for research, planning, and related activities to assess and prepare for the impacts of climate change.

topic.name: Public Safety and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety and security measures for various sectors, including maritime and land use.

Document 1: Sets forth cruise vessel security and safety requirements concerning vessel design and construction
Amends the Ports and Waterways Safety Act to revise the authority of the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to carry out a study on the acquisition of land for expansion of the Beaufort National Cemetery
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a Connecticut River Grants and Technical Assistance Program
provides grants and technical assistance to carry out projects for the conservation, restoration, and interpretation of historic and other resources

topic.name: Veteran and Community Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and resources for veterans and their communities through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security Act of 1985 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to reserve at least 15% of farmland protection program funds for grants to support cooperative efforts by an eligible state agency, county, or other eligible entity
provides with respect to cost sharing that the federal share of the purchase price of a conservation easement or other interest in eligible land shall not exceed 25%.
Document 2: Reinstates provisions of the Defense Base Closure and Realignment Act of 1990 which allow military departments to transfer surplus real and personal property at closed or realigned military installations without consideration to local redevelopment authorities
when used for economic development purposes, without the requirement to assess the property's value.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish, maintain, and modify as necessary procedures for ensuring the effective coordination of outreach activities of the Department of Veterans Affairs
Authorizes the Secretary to make grants to state or county veterans agencies for state and local outreach services.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the safety, soundness, and transparency of financial institutions and consumer protection measures in the U.S.

Document 1: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to authorize the Chairperson of the Financial Stability Oversight Council to issue a stay of, or set aside, any regulation issued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)

Requires the Council, upon the petition of a member agency of the Council, to set aside a final regulation prescribed by the CFPB if the Council decides that such regulation is inconsistent with the safe and sound operations of U.S. financial institutions.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to issue a rule within 60 days to prohibit any member of a board or body that approves permit applications for the discharge of pollutants from seeking to influence the approval of any permit in which that member has a financial interest.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to increase the total permissible amount of member business loans by an insured credit union to a limit of 25% of the credit union's total assets.

topic.name: Financial and Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to financial services and educational funding mechanisms.

Document 1: repeals a provision of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
imposes an annual fee on a health insurance provider based on its net premium income.
Document 2: promote transparency, simplicity, fairness, accountability, and equal access in the market for consumer financial products
ensure that no financial company is able to pose a systemic risk to the health of the U.S. economy by becoming too large to fail.
Document 3: allows the payment of qualified elementary and secondary education expenses from a tax-exempt qualified tuition program
increases from $2,000 to $15,000 the limit on the amount that may be contributed to a tax-exempt Coverdell education savings account.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing government accountability, reducing wasteful expenditures, and regulating agency actions.

Document 1: Requires the Comptroller General (GAO) to review executive departments to identify agencies and programs that no longer serve a public need
to report to Congress on the results of the reviews of executive departments carried out during the preceding year
Document 3: Prohibits a federal agency from proposing or finalizing any midnight rule that would likely result in significant adverse effects on competition, employment, investment, productivity, innovation
Defines 'midnight rule' as an agency statement of general applicability and future effect that is issued during the moratorium period

topic.name: Terrorism Prevention and Response
topic.description: Legislative measures focused on preventing and responding to threats posed by terrorism and related activities.

Document 1: research how commercially available small and medium sized unmanned aircraft could be used to perpetuate an attack
develop policies, guidance, and protocols for DHS to prevent, or mitigate the risks of such an attack.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any lease for federal lands to any person who conducts business with a state sponsor of terrorism
any person at least 5% of which is owned or controlled by at least one person who conducts business with a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 3: provides that the statute of limitations applicable for commencing actions by certain terrorism victims against foreign states
is an affirmative defense that is waived by such foreign state or employee if not timely raised in a responsive pleading.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the regulation, exploration, and exportation of energy resources, particularly oil and natural gas, and the promotion of renewable energy technologies.

research, development, deployment, or expansion of renewable technologies or programs that significantly decrease the use of oil.

topic.name: Organ Donation and Transplantation Policy

Facilitate timely access to donor information
Document 2: Revise the Medicare national coverage determination relating to coverage of ventricular assist devices
Extend coverage for individuals undergoing evaluation for heart transplantation
Document 3: Repeal the requirement for standards preventing acquisition of organs infected with AIDS
Authorize standards for organs infected with HIV for transplant into infected individuals

topic.name: Advertising and Consumer Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating advertising practices to protect consumers, particularly children, from misleading content.

Document 1: Truth in Advertising Act of 2014 directs the FTC to reduce the use of altered images in advertising.
Requires input from diverse stakeholders and experts in health and consumer advocacy.
Document 2: Children's Health Federal Trade Commission Authority Restoration Act repeals provisions limiting FTC's authority in children's advertising.
Allows FTC to address unfair acts in children's advertising.

topic.name: Intercollegiate and School Athletics
topic.description: The documents discuss governance, accountability, and health issues related to intercollegiate and high school athletics.

Document 1: Establishes the Presidential Commission on Intercollegiate Athletics to review, analyze, and report on issues related to intercollegiate athletics.
oversight and governance practices, health and safety protections for student athletes
Document 2: High School Athletics Accountability Act of 2007 - directs schools to report information on equality in their school athletic programs.

topic.name: Defense and Public Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses legislative measures related to defense operations, public health safety, and the ethical treatment of animals in military training.

Document 1: Prohibits either the Secretary of Defense (DOD) or the Army from using eminent domain authority to expand the Pinon Canyon Maneuver Site.
Related to defense operations and land use.
Document 2: Prohibits the inclusion of brucella abortus in lists of biological agents posing threats to public health and safety.

Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Defense to develop and validate human-based training methods for combat injury treatment.
Prohibits the use of animals in military training after a certain date.

topic.name: Health Insurance and Liability Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to health insurance provisions and liability regulations in various sectors, including health care and risk management.

Document 1: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to allow a group health plan to impose a preexisting condition exclusion only under specific circumstances.
Applies such requirement to coverage offered in the individual market.
Document 2: Amends the Liability Risk Retention Act of 1986 to require the Director of the Federal Insurance Office to report periodically on state compliance with the Act.
Imposes a fiduciary duty upon the board of directors of a risk retention group to operate in the best interests of the group.
Document 3: Requires the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to obtain consent from relevant nonfederal agencies and Indian tribes before granting a permit for a pipeline.
If the construction or operation of the pipeline has the potential to impair the beneficial uses of the surface water or groundwater.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and efficiency in federal and state government operations.

Document 3: remove restrictions on organizations influencing legislation

topic.name: Education and Policy Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving educational opportunities and addressing social issues through policy changes.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants for up to five years to eligible entities to provide eligible students with opportunity scholarships
Defines 'eligible student' as a student who is a District resident, comes from a household whose income does not exceed 300% of the poverty line
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to establish a geography education grant program
Requires program participants to use their grants and subgrants to enhance the geographic literacy of students in kindergarten through grade 12
Document 3: Directs a state to provide by law enhanced sentencing requirements for persons convicted of committing, or attempting to commit, an act of domestic violence

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Oversight

topic.name: Fishing Regulations and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to fishing practices, conservation efforts, and maritime laws affecting fishing licenses.

Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Energy to contribute energy research and development funds to the National Science Foundation.

topic.name: Education and Loan Assistance

topic.name: Environmental Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting the conservation of the environment and natural resources through various funding and grant programs.

establish a nonregulatory Delaware River Basin restoration program

incorporates such counties into the New York/New Jersey high-intensity drug trafficking area program
Document 3: TRICARE Mail-Order Pharmacy Pilot Program Act
educate and motivate beneficiaries to voluntarily increase their use of the TRICARE mail-order pharmacy

topic.name: Healthcare and Military Recruitment
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing recruitment and eligibility of healthcare professionals and cadets within military and public health service sectors.

Document 3: Prohibits the Department of Health and Human Services from using data obtained from comparative effectiveness research to deny or delay coverage
Requires HHS to ensure that comparative effectiveness research accounts for factors contributing to differences in treatment response.

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Policy Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing U.S. trade relations, immigration policy transparency, and corporate accountability.

Document 1: promote U.S. exports to PRC
provide guidance to U.S. small businesses with respect to the export of U.S. goods and services to PRC.
Document 2: submit to Congress an annual report on DHS immigration policy directives.
Document 3: acquire data regarding all administrative, civil, and criminal judicial proceedings against any corporation
prepare an annual report to Congress detailing the number of civil, administrative, and criminal enforcement actions.

topic.name: Federal Grants for Institutions
topic.description: Documents related to federal grants aimed at supporting museums, archives, and renewable energy projects.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to provide a grant to the Denver Museum of Nature & Science
Requires the Museum to match any amounts provided to the Museum under this Act.
Document 2: Requires the Archivist of the United States to make grants to states
Sets forth a formula for the allocation of grant funds to states.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers investing in wind facilities an exemption
Tax credit amounts for producing electricity from wind facilities.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, access, and use of federal lands and resources in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretaries of the Interior or Agriculture from taking any action that would permanently close, restrict, or render or declare unserviceable any aircraft landing strip located on land under their jurisdiction
Requires the Secretaries to establish a nationwide policy for governing backcountry aviation issues relating to the management of federal land
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer to the state of Alaska all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the 1002 Area of Alaska
Prohibits export from the United States of any oil produced in the 1002 Area after the date of transfer
Document 3: Reserves for 20 years certain federal land for use by the Department of Homeland Security
for the Federal Law Enforcement Training Center in Eddy County, New Mexico
